consistency.attribute_completeness: Complete. All standard spell attributes (level, school, casting time, range, components, duration, description, classes) are present.

quality.description_clarity: High clarity. Describes the trigger (failed save), the initial effect (frightened condition), and the recurring damage effect clearly.
quality.mechanical_ambiguity: Low ambiguity. The relationship between the frightened condition and the recurring save/damage is explicit: frightened for duration, OR until they succeed on the recurring Wisdom save.
quality.component_check: Components (V, S) are standard for a high-level spell.

mechanics.level_school_match: Level 9 Illusion is appropriate for a very powerful, mind-affecting, area-of-effect control spell.
mechanics.area_of_effect: 30-foot-radius sphere, centered within 120 feet. Standard radius/range parameters.
mechanics.saving_throw: Initial Wisdom save to resist being frightened. This is consistent for fear/illusion effects.
mechanics.duration_and_concentration: Concentration, up to 1 minute. Standard for ongoing mental effects.
mechanics.damage_scaling: 4d10 psychic damage at the end of the frightened creature's turn. This is significant damage for a persistent, ongoing effect, balanced by requiring the creature to remain frightened.
mechanics.ending_condition: The spell ends for a creature on a successful Wisdom save during its turn. This provides the creature an opportunity to break free each turn, preventing permanent lockdown via failed saves, which is good design.
